data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Google employs a microservices architecture to enhance scalability and manageability.
data.technical_spec: Services are decoupled, ensuring efficient scaling, independent deployment, and fault isolation.

data.title: Data Center Infrastructure
data.description: Google uses highly advanced data centers to host its services worldwide.
data.technical_spec: Equipped with advanced cooling, distributed systems, and proprietary hardware.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Efficient global load balancing ensures minimal response time and reliability.
data.technical_spec: Geographic spread, traffic routing, and redundancy handling.

data.title: Kubernetes Orchestration
data.description: Google orchestrates workloads using Kubernetes to manage containerized applications.
data.technical_spec: Automation of deploys, scaling, and operations of application containers.

data.title: Cloud Infrastructure
data.description: Leveraging robust cloud capabilities to support diverse service offerings.
data.technical_spec: Google Cloud Platform (GCP) provides computation, storage, analytics, and AI.
